description_long: This object is a stylized violin with a geometric, angular body finished in a medium brown color, resembling wood. The body is octagonal rather than traditionally curved, with a flat top and back, and lacks the characteristic f-holes typically found on violins. The neck and fingerboard are black and extend from the body, leading to a simple pegbox without a visible scroll. Four strings are stretched from a rectangular tailpiece at the base of the body up to the pegbox, but there is no visible chinrest. The overall design is minimalist, with no fine detailing or ornamentation, and the surface appears smooth and hard.
description: A stylized, angular brown violin with a black neck and four visible strings, lacking f-holes and a chinrest. The body is octagonal and the surface is smooth and hard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the violin, with the black neck and brown body visible in cross-section.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the flat, octagonal brown back of the violin and the black neck extending upward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the layered construction of the body and neck.
description_view_3: Front view revealing the four black strings, the rectangular tailpiece, and the flat, angular brown body without f-holes or chinrest.
